title: Validation of a Yeast-Based Functional Assay
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8vd5","text":"Yeast-based functional complementation assays are a powerful, scalable (amendable to variant effect mapping) approach for evaluating the functional impact of human genetic variants. These assays are based on a classic strategy in which fitness defects caused by inactivating mutations in endogenous yeast genes are rescued by expression of the corresponding human ortholog. The functional effects of human variants are assessed by their ability to restore growth relative to the wild-type allele. This protocol describes the setup and confirmation/validation of such an assay.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"21r9k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9tqdu","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8b0qj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6d9hj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Millipore Sigma: GROW-MRGF-4240-10L)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bkpk1","text":"Dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO; Millipore Sigma: 472301-100ML)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1d70b","text":"Cell Culture Dishes (ThermoFisher Scientific: 150460)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bhd17","text":"Yeast Transformation Frozen-EZ Yeast Transformation II Kit (Zymo: T2001)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bm5ng","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Yeast Transformation Two alternative options (1.1 and 1.2) for yeast transformations:

Yeast Spotting Single colonies (for wildtype, empty-vector and variant) isolated in duplicate from transformation plates Colonies inoculated, then grown in permissive media at 30°C until saturation Cultures diluted to an OD 600 = 0.3 and grown until all reached OD 600 = ~0.8 - 1.2 Cultures then centrifuged for 4 min at 500xg, Wash the pellet three times with 5 mL of sterile distilled water Resuspended washed pellet in 1 ml of liquid restrictive/permissive media. Resuspended pellets are then serially diluted (e.g., 5 −1 , 5 −2 , 5 −3 , 5 −4 , and 5 −5 ) 5μL or 10μL of each dilution are spotted on plates (restrictive or permissive media) Incubate for a minimum of 48 hours at restrictive or permissive temperature

LiAc Approach for Yeast Transformation Transformation buffer: %s %s * Mix 500 mg PEG3350 with 600μL H 2 O, keep at 60°C for 5 minutes, vortex several times, the final volume will be 1 ml, filterize to sterilize ** Prepare enough for the variant, a negative (empty-vector) and positive (wildtype) control Mix 50μL transformation buffer and 2-5 ul plasmid in a 96-well PCR plate Inoculate fresh yeast colonies and mix by pipetting up and down Incubate at room temperature for 2 hours (mandatory) Incubate 4°C overnight (can also be done for minimum of 3 hours) Heat Shock at 42°C in a PCR machine for 30 minutes Ice immediately after for 10 minutes Spread on an appropriate plate Incubate the plates at 30°C for 2-4 days to allow for growth of transformants.

Performed with Frozen-EZ Yeast Transformation II Kit (T2001, Zymo) Protocol * Strain Information: If your yeast strain is temperature-sensitive, use 25°C for all steps, otherwise use 30°C. Day 1: Inoculate 5 ml of medium with yeast and shake at 200 rpm at 25°C or 30°C overnight. Day 2: Measure the OD 600 of the overnight yeast culture. Dilute yeast cultures to 4 different starting ODs each in 200 ml of medium in 500 ml flasks and shake at 200 rpm at 25°C or 30°C overnight. Example: OD = 0.06, OD = 0.03, OD = 0.015, OD = 0.005 Day 3: Measure the OD 600 of each flask and use the culture closest to OD = 0.8-1.2 for the remaining steps. Pellet the cells at 500 x g for 4 minutes and discard the supernatant. Add 10 ml Frozen-EZ Yeast Solution 1 to wash the pellet. Re-pellet the cells and discard the supernatant. Add 1 ml Frozen-EZ Yeast Solution 2 to resuspend the pellet. Mix 50µl of competent cells with 0.2-1 µg plasmid DNA (in less than 5 µl volume) Add 500µl Frozen-EZ Yeast Solution 3 and mix thoroughly by pipetting. Incubate at 30°C for 45 minutes - Mix occasionally by flicking Spread 50-150 µl of the transformation mixture on an appropriate plate. Spread on an appropriate plate Incubate the plates at 30°C for 2-4 days to allow for growth of transformants.
creator: Warren van Loggerenberg
